claimText: The International Booker Prize is an international literary award hosted in the United Kingdom.
evidenceExcerpt: The International Booker Prize is an international literary award hosted in the United Kingdom.
examUse: Could support a statement-based or institution-feature question.

claimText: * The introduction of the International Prize to complement the Man Booker Prize, as the Booker Prize was then known, was announced in June 2004.
evidenceExcerpt: * The introduction of the International Prize to complement the Man Booker Prize, as the Booker Prize was then known, was announced in June 2004.
examUse: Could support a statement-based or institution-feature question.
Could support a statement-based or institution-feature question.

claimText: * Since 2016, the award has been given annually to a single work of fiction or collection of short stories, translated into English and published in the United Kingdom or Ireland, with a £50,000 prize for the winning title, shared equally between author and translator.
evidenceExcerpt: * Since 2016, the award has been given annually to a single work of fiction or collection of short stories, translated into English and published in the United Kingdom or Ireland, with a £50,000 prize for the winning title, shared equally between author and translator.
